quarters.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd. reported a strong Q1 FY26-27 performance driven by oil prices, Jio, and Retail, despite macroeconomic challenges. The company is focusing on digital scale in Retail and maintaining operational resilience in Energy/O2C amid geopolitical volatility.

subsections.label: Consolidated performance and what drove it
points: Management framed the quarter as 'extraordinary' due to macro volatility, energy market shock, and supply chain dislocation.
Revenue increased by 25% YoY, driven by oil prices, Jio, and Retail.
EBITDA exceeded Rs. 54,000 Cr, a 10% increase.
PAT was approximately Rs. 23,200 Cr, a 6% increase.
Consumer businesses now constitute about 50% of the overall mix.
Capex was funded by cash profit, with net debt slightly lower QoQ at Rs. 1.23 lakh Cr.
Q1 capex was approximately Rs. 39,000 Cr.
Moody's upgraded RIL to Baa1 and S&P to A-.
Finance cost increased by 19% and depreciation by 9% due to Jio capitalization.

subsections.label: Oil-to-Chemicals (Refining & Marketing + Petrochem)
points: O2C revenue increased by 30% YoY; EBITDA increased by 17% to approximately Rs. 17,000 Cr.
Headline cracks/deltas overstate realizable economics due to shock costs.
RIL maintained 96-97% throughput despite logistics/availability crisis.
Middle East production fell by about 12 million bpd during this period.
RIL diversified crude sources and optimized trade/logistics.
Gas diversion to priority sectors forced more liquids, but RIL maximized gasifier throughput.
SAED drag and domestic under-recoveries were headwinds.
Planned turnaround lowered production meant for sale by 10%.
LPG production increased almost fourfold, impacting petchem feed slate.
High cracks but demand destruction outside India; India demand remained strong.

subsections.label: Petrochemicals
points: Quarter described as a roller coaster with crude changing 5-10% in a day.
Naphtha increased by 61% to $903/ton, with premiums and freight spikes.
Middle East PE supplies blocked, leading to PE delta surge.
Polyester deltas rose despite MEG delta declining.
Polymer demand was down 22%; polyester downstream hit by gas curtailment and labor disruption.
RIL's ethane advantage widened due to cheaper US associated gas.
Ethane logistics improved with new VLECs, increasing ethane availability.

subsections.label: Exploration & Production
points: Revenue increased by 3.2% YoY; EBITDA was almost flat/slightly lower.
KGD6 production and price realization were lower due to ceiling price.
CBM production crossed 1 million standard cubic meters of gas with better realizations.
Condensate realizations were high at approximately $107/bbl.
Rig arriving next month for KG basin exploration.
CBM continues 40-well program and multilaterals across both blocks.
KGD6 ceiling expected to rise to ~$9.9 in 2H.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms
points: Subscribers reached 533m; net add 35.2m in 12 months.
5G users reached 285m; home broadband 28.6m, with 14m AirFiber.
JPL revenue was Rs. 39,173 Cr, EBITDA Rs. 20,865 Cr, margin 53.3%.
Usage was 69.4 exabytes, with per-capita 43.7 GB/user/month.
ARPU was Rs. 215.6, with management denying underlying pressure.
Digital services revenue growth was ~20% YoY, outpacing connectivity.
Enterprise push is managed services-led, enabled by FWA.
78% FWA market share of net additions in India.
JPL PAT was Rs. 764 Cr despite strong EBITDA due to accounting shift.

subsections.label: Reliance Retail
points: Q1 revenue was approximately Rs. 90,000 Cr; underlying growth was 11.6%.
Customers increased by 11%; transactions by 46% vs revenue 12%.
Grocery digital orders increased by 116% YoY.
EBITDA was Rs. 6,309 Cr, slightly down YoY; margin was 7.9%.
Three-year roadmap focuses on digital commerce and hyperlocal delivery.
Quick commerce differentiation claims include transaction data and physical footprint leverage.
Grocery: 1,000+ hypermarkets; LFL +7%.
Electronics: LFL +16%; ResQ +27%.
Fashion: LFL +4%; Ajio Rush orders +136% QoQ.
RCPL revenue was Rs. 8,600 Cr, with rapid scale and supply-chain buildout.
RCPL claims #3 NARTD player in India, with plans for international expansion.

subsections.label: JioStar (Media)
points: Platform crossed 530m users; IPL reach 700m; CTV consumption +19%.
Tadka micro-content hub engaged 100m users in ~2 months.
Deep integration with OpenAI for conversational discovery.
JAMS in-house AI media studio launched AI-generated micro-content.
First in-app commerce integration with Swiggy.
Revenue was Rs. 10,946 Cr, with PBT also +14%.
Advertising hit by real-money gaming ban and US-Iran war impact.

subsections.label: Digital services and enterprise strategy
points: Digital services revenue growth was ~20% YoY, outpacing connectivity.
Enterprise push is managed services-led, bundling connectivity with security, managed Wi-Fi, ERP, analytics, and surveillance.
FWA leadership with 78% market share of net additions in India.
Patent position: only Indian tech innovator in WIPO PCT global top-20 with around 4,500 patents.

subsections.label: FWA leadership and technology/IP
points: 78% FWA market share of net additions in India.
Patent position: only Indian tech innovator in WIPO PCT global top-20 with around 4,500 patents.

subsections.label: JioStar (Media)
points: Platform crossed 530m users; IPL reach 700m; CTV consumption +19%.
Tadka micro-content hub engaged 100m users in ~2 months.
Deep integration with OpenAI for conversational discovery.
JAMS in-house AI media studio launched AI-generated micro-content.
First in-app commerce integration with Swiggy.

subsections.label: Petrochemicals
points: RIL's ethane advantage widened due to cheaper US associated gas.
Ethane logistics improved with new VLECs, increasing ethane availability.
Current ethylene feed mix: ethane and ROGC constitute about 70% of ethylene.

subsections.label: RCPL (FMCG)
points: RCPL revenue was Rs. 8,600 Cr, with rapid scale and supply-chain buildout.
RCPL claims #3 NARTD player in India, with plans for international expansion.
Manufacturing: commissioned (partially) one of the largest beverage plants in Asia.
M&A/brand integration: majority stake in Sosyo JV; operational transition completed for Toni & Guy, Brylcreem, Badedas, Matey.

subsections.label: Headwinds called out explicitly
points: SAED drag and domestic under-recoveries were headwinds.
Planned turnaround lowered production meant for sale by 10%.
LPG production increased almost fourfold, impacting petchem feed slate.
Advertising hit by real-money gaming ban and US-Iran war impact.

subsections.label: Forward view (management tone constructive but contingent)
points: Structural support for cracks from lost capacity (Middle East outages) and Russia.
Management refused near-term profitability calls due to renewed SOH volatility.
KGD6 ceiling expected to rise to ~$9.9 in 2H.

guidance_ledger.statement: KGD6 ceiling expected to rise to ~$9.9 in 2H.
Expecting to ramp up the rate of connecting new homes significantly.
On track to complete 40 gigawatt hours of battery energy storage gigafactory at Jamnagar.
Scaling up to 20 gigawatt of solar PV module production completely vertically integrated.

risk_register.risk: Planned turnaround lowered production meant for sale by 10%
LPG production increased almost fourfold, impacting petchem feed slate
Advertising hit by real-money gaming ban and US-Iran war impact

risk_register.note: 11% decline for the year and 4% in March.
Impact on industry and consumer confidence.
Middle East tensions affecting oil supply and prices.
Impact on business needs to be managed.

risk_register.risk: Natural decline in KG D6 fields
risk_register.note: Efforts underway to augment production.
Prices moved from $11 to $9.5, impacting realizations.
Affecting CBM production.
Due to geopolitical tensions and OPEC Plus actions.
In global Naphtha cracker operating rates.

themes.label: Subscriber growth
themes.note: Jio Platforms added 35.2m subscribers in 12 months, with 5G users reaching 285m.

themes.label: Retail expansion
themes.note: Reliance Retail focuses on digital commerce and hyperlocal delivery with a three-year roadmap.

themes.label: Oil & Gas challenges
themes.note: RIL maintained high throughput despite logistics crises, but faced headwinds from SAED drag and LPG production impacts.

themes.label: AI initiatives
themes.note: JioStar launched AI-generated micro-content and integrated with OpenAI for conversational discovery.

themes.label: New Energy development
themes.note: KGD6 ceiling expected to rise, but management refused near-term profitability calls due to volatility.

key_takeaways: RIL is explicitly trading near-term consumer margins for digital scale (Retail) and building new infrastructure moats (dark stores; data centers; AI/media tooling).

In Energy/O2C, management stresses realizable margin is constrained by procurement/logistics friction even when market cracks are extreme; nonetheless, RIL demonstrated operational resilience (near-full runs) and feedstock advantage (ethane-heavy slate).

Forward commentary is deliberately non-committal on near-term O2C earnings due to geopolitical volatility ('a lot of imponderables'), but management maintains confidence in structural positioning: 'structurally… we are in a good place right through.'

quarters.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd anticipates continued growth across its segments, with a focus on expanding its digital services, retail, and new energy initiatives, despite challenges in the oil and gas sector due to geopolitical tensions.

subsections.label: Group Performance
points: The first 11 months showed robust domestic activity and significant growth in telecom traffic on the 5G network.
Rupee depreciation was a concern, with an 11% decline for the year and 4% in March.
Revenue was up 10% for FY2025-26, with EBITDA up 13.5%, including a one-time gain from the sale of listed shares.
Consumer businesses contributed more than 55% of EBITDA.
JPL's PAT increased by 15% year-on-year, RRVL by 12%, and RIL by 24%.
Overall growth was 13%, with digital services showing strong growth at 18%.
Retail EBITDA growth was 8%, muted by the scale-up of hyperlocal e-commerce.
O2C grew by 10% despite challenges, and Oil and Gas faced production declines.
For the quarter, EBITDA growth was flat, with consumer segments (Jio and retail) up 14%.
Net debt remained controlled, with a net debt to EBITDA ratio of 0.64.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms
points: Jio Platforms ended the year with 524 million subscribers, adding 36.3 million during the year.
The 5G user base reached 268 million, with 77 million additions during the year.
Jio Platforms revenue for the year was Rs.1,46,085 Crores, up 14.6% year-on-year.
EBITDA was Rs.76,255 Crores, with a margin of 52%, showing a 190-basis point improvement.
Total data traffic increased to 241 Exabytes for the year, a 31% increase year-on-year.

subsections.label: Retail
points: Retail business achieved the highest ever revenues of Rs.98,000 Crores for the quarter.
EBITDA for the quarter was Rs.6,900 Crores, with a margin of 7.9%.
Hyperlocal commerce showed a 30% growth in average daily orders quarter-on-quarter.
The number of registered customers grew by 11% year-on-year, with transactions up 39%.
333 new stores were opened during the quarter, crossing 20,000 stores in total.
Grocery and fashion segments showed strong double-digit growth.
JioMart serves 1200+ cities with 3100 stores, offering two-hour delivery.

subsections.label: FMCG
points: FMCG business closed with a revenue of Rs.22,000 Crores in 2026, doubling from the previous year.
Campa brand delivered Rs.4700 Crores in revenue, becoming the fourth largest carbonated soft drink brand in India.
Independence brand delivered Rs.2600 Crores in revenue, becoming a promising and trusted brand.
The package drinking water business became the third largest in India.
International presence expanded to 40 countries.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms Technology Initiatives
points: Jio Platforms is implementing AI-first network optimizations, including energy optimization.
Network innovations include proprietary beam form cell design to improve coverage and capacity.
Distribution strategy involves working with OEMs to enhance user experience with new devices.
Customer experience improvements have led to Jio being rated as the best network by third-party reports.

subsections.label: JioStar Technology Initiatives
points: JioStar launched micro-content called Tadka, integrating horizontal and vertical content.
Deep product integration with OpenAI's ChatGPT transformed search functionality within the app.
In-app commerce integration with Swiggy allows users to order food during live events.

subsections.label: FMCG Manufacturing
points: FMCG is investing in manufacturing capabilities to become one of the largest manufacturers of cooldrinks in India.
The company is setting up integrated food parks to drive cost efficiencies and integrated operations.
FMCG has 12 plants across India and plans to expand further as the business grows.

subsections.label: Geopolitical and Economic Risks
points: Rupee depreciation is a concern, with an 11% decline for the year and 4% in March.
Supply shocks and their impact on industry and consumer confidence are major concerns.
Geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East, have affected oil supply and prices.
The introduction of SAED and its impact on the business is a risk that needs to be managed.

subsections.label: Future Outlook
points: Reliance Industries anticipates continued growth across its segments, with a focus on expanding digital services, retail, and new energy initiatives.
The company is optimistic about the resilience of demand for its products, despite geopolitical challenges.

guidance_ledger.statement: Expecting to ramp up the rate of connecting new homes significantly.
On track to complete 40 gigawatt hours of battery energy storage gigafactory at Jamnagar.
Scaling up to 20 gigawatt of solar PV module production completely vertically integrated.

risk_register.note: 11% decline for the year and 4% in March.
Impact on industry and consumer confidence.
Middle East tensions affecting oil supply and prices.
Impact on business needs to be managed.

risk_register.risk: Natural decline in KG D6 fields
risk_register.note: Efforts underway to augment production.
Prices moved from $11 to $9.5, impacting realizations.
Affecting CBM production.
Due to geopolitical tensions and OPEC Plus actions.
In global Naphtha cracker operating rates.

themes.label: Subscriber growth
themes.note: Jio Platforms added 36.3 million subscribers, reaching 524 million.

themes.label: Retail expansion
themes.note: Retail business achieved highest ever revenues with significant store expansion.

themes.label: New Energy development
themes.note: Significant progress in green ammonia supply contracts and solar generation projects.

themes.label: Oil & Gas challenges
themes.note: Geopolitical tensions and supply shocks affecting oil supply and prices.

themes.label: AI initiatives
themes.note: Jio Platforms and JioStar implementing AI-driven technologies for network and content enhancements.

key_takeaways: The first 11 months showed robust domestic activity and significant growth in telecom traffic on the 5G network.
Rupee depreciation was a concern, with an 11% decline for the year and 4% in March.
Jio Platforms ended the year with 524 million subscribers, adding 36.3 million during the year.
Retail business achieved the highest ever revenues of Rs.98,000 Crores for the quarter.
FMCG business closed with a revenue of Rs.22,000 Crores in 2026, doubling from the previous year.
Geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East, have affected oil supply and prices.
Reliance Industries anticipates continued growth across its segments, with a focus on expanding digital services, retail, and new energy initiatives.
pressing_questions: How much of distillate yield can actually change if alternate crudes continue to be the only option?
Is there a level at which price increases are considered for fuel retail due to significant losses?
How comfortable is the company in managing procurement, freight, and other costs in Q1?
What percentage of production is impacted by SAED?
What are the opportunities in terms of increasing realization or subscribers through network slicing in telecom?
How does the company see the quick commerce industry consolidating in the next few years?

quarters.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd continues to see strong growth across its diversified businesses, with significant progress in new energy initiatives and robust performance in digital services and retail.

subsections.label: Group Performance
points: Revenue is up 10%, EBITDA up about 6%, and PAT at 22,290 crores, which was also up about 1.6%.
Revenue has been led by digital services and retail, with EBITDA driven by strong O2C performance, 15% higher.
Balance sheet remains steady with no change in net debt number, leverage, etc., and businesses continue to deliver cash.
S&P changed the rating from BBB Plus to A Minus, focusing on higher earnings from less cyclical businesses.

subsections.label: Retail Performance
points: Retail business had the highest ever revenue at 97,600 Crores, up 8.1%.
EBITDA came in at 6,915 Crores with a margin of 8%.
Hyperlocal commerce is scaling up rapidly with 1.6 million orders and 360% Y-O-Y growth.
Entered into an exclusive partnership with Fabletics, an American women's athleisure brand.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms Performance
points: Jio Platforms ended the quarter with 515 million plus subscribers, 8.9 million net adds.
5G user base growing rapidly with 253 million plus 5G subscribers.
Revenues crossed 37,000 Crores with an EBITDA margin of 52%.
5G deployment helping win more customers with 65% share of 5G subscribers in India.

subsections.label: FMCG Performance
points: RCPL became a direct subsidiary of RIL from 1st December 2025, adding 5,000 Crores as revenue.
YTD numbers stand at 15,000 Crores.
Daily essentials grew 1.5 times Y-O-Y, and the Independence brand crossed 1,500.
Energy portfolio reached 1,000 Crores in the quarter.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms Technology
points: Developed proprietary technologies across the network, with 4G and 5G running on in-house stack.
World's largest fixed wireless operator with in-house developed solutions.
Home OS and tele OS offered to homes on connected devices, scaled to over 25 million homes.
Partnerships like JioHotstar and Google Gemini Pro enhancing customer offerings.

subsections.label: New Energy Manufacturing
points: Commissioning of the first fully integrated 10-gigawatt peak annual solar manufacturing gigafactory is on track.
Solar module manufacturing has reached a high yield of 94%-95%.
Battery manufacturing is progressing with a 40-gigawatt hour capacity, expanding to 100-gigawatt hour.
Construction at Kutch for solar power generation is in full swing, with significant land development completed.

subsections.label: Oil & Gas Challenges
points: Natural decline in KG D6 fields, efforts underway to augment production.
LNG prices have moved from $11 to $9.5, impacting overall realizations.
CBM productivity improved by almost three-and-a-half to four times in recent campaigns.

subsections.label: Future Outlook
points: Strong growth expected in digital services and retail, driven by technology and innovation.
Continued focus on expanding new energy initiatives and integrated manufacturing capabilities.
Emphasis on maintaining strong cash generation and balance sheet stability.

guidance_ledger.statement: Expecting to ramp up the rate of connecting new homes significantly.
On track to complete 40 gigawatt hours of battery energy storage gigafactory at Jamnagar.
Scaling up to 20 gigawatt of solar PV module production completely vertically integrated.

risk_register.risk: Natural decline in KG D6 fields
risk_register.note: Efforts underway to augment production.
Prices moved from $11 to $9.5, impacting realizations.
Affecting CBM production.
Due to geopolitical tensions and OPEC Plus actions.
In global Naphtha cracker operating rates.

themes.label: Subscriber growth
themes.note: Jio Platforms continues to see strong subscriber growth with 515 million plus subscribers.

themes.label: Retail expansion
themes.note: Retail business achieved highest ever revenue with significant growth in hyperlocal commerce.

themes.label: New Energy development
themes.note: Significant progress in solar and battery manufacturing with integrated facilities on track.

themes.label: Oil & Gas challenges
themes.note: Natural decline in KG D6 fields and LNG price decline impacting realizations.

themes.label: AI initiatives
themes.note: Jio Platforms leveraging AI-enabled smart factories and proprietary technologies.

key_takeaways: Customer addition strong at about 9 million, taking the total to about 515 million customers.
Retail revenue growth has been about 8%, and overall EBITDA growth has been 1.3%.
FMCG turnover at more than 5,000 Crores, 60% up on a year-on-year basis.
Jio-bp continues to do well with 24% growth in volumes for both gas oil and gasoline.
Revenue is up 10%, EBITDA up about 6%, and overall, PAT at 22,290 crores, which was also up about 1.6%.
S&P changed our rating from BBB Plus to A Minus, focusing on higher earnings from less cyclical businesses.
Jio Platforms ended the quarter with 515 million plus subscribers, 8.9 million net adds during the quarter.
RCPL became the direct subsidiary of RIL from 1st December 2025, adding 5,000 Crores as revenue.

quarters.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd reported strong performance across its businesses, with significant growth in Jio Platforms, Retail, and New Energy segments, while maintaining a stable balance sheet and expanding its strategic initiatives.

subsections.label: Group Performance
points: Strong performance across businesses with improving quality of numbers.
EBITDA margin expansion in Jio and Retail segments.
Retail revenues higher by about 18% with significant growth in fashion, lifestyle, grocery, and electronics.
Energy business saw a 21% increase due to higher cracks across gasoline, gas oil, and ATF.
Domestic placements of gasoline and gas oil up 34%.
Upstream EBITDA stable at about 5,000 Crores despite natural production decline.
New energy initiatives on track with panel production and development of RERTC.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms
points: 506 million subscribers with 8.3 million net additions.
234 million 5G users with 21 million net additions.
36,000 Crores plus revenue with 52% EBITDA margin.
Strong growth in non-urban areas for 5G services.
JioAirFiber homes reached 9.5 million, making it the world's largest fixed broadband service provider.

subsections.label: Reliance Retail
points: 18% YOY revenue growth with strong performance across consumption baskets.
400 plus new stores added during the quarter.
Quick commerce offering scaling up aggressively with 6 million new transacting customers.
Fashion business with strong LFL growth and expansion of premium formats.
Ajio Rush offering quick commerce in 300 plus pin codes.
FMCG business doubled with strong market share gains for Campa and Independence brands.

subsections.label: Media & Entertainment
points: 400 million monthly active users on JioHotstar.
54% growth in watch time for Big Boss across languages.
Special Ops became the second highest watched series on the platform.
JioHotstar is the largest sports platform with significant viewership for India-England series.
Record EBITDA performance with industry-leading margins of 28.1%.

subsections.label: Jio Platforms Technology
points: Proprietary 5G stack and fixed wireless stack operating at global scale.
3400 plus patent applications across 5G and 6G.
Development of JioTeleOS for set-top boxes and TVs.
Jio PC and JioFrames launched with positive market feedback.
AI initiatives with Reliance Intelligence focusing on infrastructure and product development.

subsections.label: New Energy Manufacturing
points: Construction of the world's largest new energy complex in Jamnagar.
Commissioning of solar PV module lines and upcoming solar cell gigafactories.
Development of battery energy storage system gigafactories.
Integrated manufacturing ecosystem for solar and battery production.

subsections.label: Strategic Partnerships and Initiatives
points: NCLT approval for de-merger expected in November.
Partnerships with Stella McCartney and Max & Co. in premium brands business.
Reliance Intelligence formed as a 100% subsidiary to develop AI capabilities.
Joint venture with Meta for enterprise solutions.
Expansion of Jio-bp network and new mobility solutions.

subsections.label: Market and External Risks
points: Natural decline in KG-D6 fields impacting oil & gas segment.
Adverse weather conditions affecting CBM production.
Volatility in crude prices due to geopolitical tensions and OPEC Plus actions.
Weak demand and overcapacity in global Naphtha cracker operating rates.

subsections.label: Future Outlook
points: Continued focus on expanding Jio's subscriber base and connectivity solutions.
Retail segment to leverage omnichannel capabilities and expand premium formats.
New Energy segment to scale up renewable energy and battery production.
AI initiatives to develop and monetize new products and solutions.

guidance_ledger.statement: Expecting to ramp up the rate of connecting new homes significantly.
On track to complete 40 gigawatt hours of battery energy storage gigafactory at Jamnagar.
Scaling up to 20 gigawatt of solar PV module production completely vertically integrated.

risk_register.note: Impacting oil & gas segment.
Affecting CBM production.
Due to geopolitical tensions and OPEC Plus actions.
In global Naphtha cracker operating rates.

themes.label: Subscriber growth
themes.note: Jio Platforms achieved 506 million subscribers with significant growth in 5G users.

themes.label: Retail expansion
themes.note: Retail segment reported 18% YOY revenue growth with expansion in quick commerce and premium formats.

themes.label: New Energy development
themes.note: Progressing with integrated manufacturing ecosystem for solar and battery.

themes.label: AI initiatives
themes.note: Reliance Intelligence focusing on infrastructure and product development.

themes.label: Oil & Gas challenges
themes.note: Natural decline in KG-D6 fields impacting segment performance.

key_takeaways: Strong performance across businesses with improving quality of numbers.
Jio Platforms achieved 506 million subscribers with significant growth in 5G users.
Retail segment reported 18% YOY revenue growth with expansion in quick commerce and premium formats.
Media & Entertainment segment maintained 400 million monthly active users with strong content lineup.
New Energy initiatives progressing with integrated manufacturing ecosystem for solar and battery.
AI initiatives with Reliance Intelligence focusing on infrastructure and product development.
Natural decline in KG-D6 fields impacting oil & gas segment.
pressing_questions: Should we expect more tariff hikes in the near term for Jio?
Are we close to the peak in terms of monthly run rate for connecting new homes?
What is the role of Jio in the Reliance Intelligence initiative?
Any commercial development to talk about in the tech stack globally?
Any broad understanding in terms of capex investments for Reliance Intelligence?

quarters.summary: Infosys anticipates a challenging FY27 with revenue growth guidance of 1.5%-3.5% CC YoY, factoring in macroeconomic headwinds and strategic decisions to not pursue certain deals. The company remains focused on AI-driven growth and strategic partnerships, while managing risks from geopolitical tensions and competitive pressures.

subsections.label: Margins
points: FY26 operating margin was 21%; Q4 was 20.9%.
Gains from currency and Project Maximus were reinvested in talent, AI, and sales and marketing.

subsections.label: Cash & Working Capital
points: DSO was 78, the lowest in 7 years.
FY26 FCF was $3.5bn (adjusted), with FCF conversion at 106% for the year and 111% for Q4.

subsections.label: Capital Return
points: Returned over $4 bn to shareholders in FY26.
Proposed final dividend of INR 25, total INR 48, which is a 11.6% increase YoY.

subsections.label: Large Deals
points: FY26 TCV for large deals was $14.9–$15.0bn, a 28% YoY increase, with 55% net new.
Q4 TCV was $3.2bn across 19 large deals.
Management emphasized that the large deal pipeline continues to remain strong.

subsections.label: Vertical/Geography Mix
points: Outperformance in Financial Services, Communications, Manufacturing, and Europe.
Communications, Manufacturing, and Europe grew more than double the company average, led by large deal wins.

subsections.label: Financial Services
points: FS grew 4.4% in FY26, driven by large-deal ramps, AI-led transformation, legacy modernization, and vendor consolidation.
Market sentiment remains positive with continued consumer spending across US banking, capital markets, and Europe.
CY26 budgets are expected to grow in the US.
Infosys is a strategic AI partner for 18 out of the top 20 clients.
Signed a large GCC deal for a regional bank in the US and a large AI-first GCC deal.

subsections.label: EURS (Energy/Utilities/Resources/Services)
points: Demand is constructive, supported by a strong large deal pipeline and vendor consolidation driven by cost takeout.
Energy sector sees increased outsourcing leading to healthy deal momentum.
Utilities face structurally higher demand due to grid constraints, renewables integration, and acceleration of electricity needs for data centers.
80% of the large deal TCV of FY26 was net new, positioned as a driver of FY27 acceleration.

subsections.label: Manufacturing
points: Cautious amid softer demand, particularly in automotive and parts of Europe.
Tariffs and ongoing Middle East conflict are causing delayed decisions.
Focus on cost optimization and operational resilience.
Near term and FY27 growth will be impacted due to low revenue from one large client.

subsections.label: Retail & Communications
points: Retail budgets are tightly controlled, with discretionary spending under pressure.
Clients want AI productivity to do more with similar budgets.
Communications budgets are flat to negative, with discretionary spending impacted.
Shift from Generative to Agentic AI, with clients consolidating IT+BPM to cut costs.
Uptick in AI deals in IT operations, software replacement, and mainframe migration.

subsections.label: AI Strategy
points: Infosys reiterated its AI thesis from Investor Day, highlighting a large addressable market for AI services across six areas: AI strategy & engineering, data, process, legacy modernization, physical AI, and trust.
Core platforms Topaz Fabric (AI) and Cobalt (cloud) are operational today.
Management pushed back on 'pilot-only' narratives, citing production-scale examples.

subsections.label: Proof Points
points: Ralph Lauren: conversational/personalized AI tool increased revenue by 12% and customer engagement by 50%.
Hertz: migrated 3 mn lines of COBOL code to microservices using foundation models, reducing cost by 60% and timeline by 60%.
BP: 50 AI agent initiatives across multiple functions, achieving 95% payment accuracy, 50% faster contract validation, and 18% improvement in IT operations efficiency.

subsections.label: Partner Ecosystem and Internal Rollout
points: New partnerships with OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, NVIDIA, Microsoft, AWS, Google Cloud, and Intel.
30,000+ developers on GitHub Copilot.
90%+ of employees skilled on different AI platforms.
Fresher program includes tiered compensation for AI-aligned skills and a 'forward deployed engineer' construct for client-facing AI build work.

subsections.label: AI Revenue Disclosure
points: AI contribution is above the prior disclosed 5.5%, but management withheld updated percentages.
Management shared 5.5% at Investor Day to show materiality and growth, but are not sharing updated numbers now.

subsections.label: AI Spend Compression vs Growth
points: Management described a two-sided ledger: compression in some tech services and BPM due to efficient foundation models/tooling, and growth in AI-first work, net new large deals, and footprint expansion.
Productivity pass-through is acknowledged as a margin headwind, but growth currently exceeds compression.

subsections.label: Acquisitions/JV in Guidance
points: Stratus (insurance-related) is included in guidance, contributing ~25 bps to FY27 growth.
Optimum Healthcare and Versent JV are excluded as they are pending regulatory approvals.

subsections.label: Geopolitics
points: Management acknowledged a change in economic environment after Middle East/Iran conflict escalation, but sees paths towards stabilization.
Macro stability is tied to tech spend recovery.

subsections.label: Competitive Intensity & 'Irrational' Bids
points: Management acknowledged occasional competitor proposals outside the range of what models can deliver.
Infosys may walk away from deals not aligned with ROIC discipline, as seen in the European manufacturing case.

subsections.label: AI Security/Vulnerability Angle (Mythos)
points: CEO described Mythos as exposing more vulnerabilities than expected, framing it as a services opportunity to help clients protect vulnerabilities.

subsections.label: Leadership/Succession Questions
points: CEO gave 'No comment' on tenure/succession-related queries.

subsections.label: FY27 Outlook
points: Revenue guidance is 1.5%–3.5% CC YoY, with the lower end assuming higher deterioration in the environment and the upper end assuming an improved environment.
Operating margin guidance is 20%–22%.
Guidance includes contribution from Stratus but excludes Optimum Healthcare and Versent JV.
Known headwind of 0.75%–1% revenue reduction due to lower revenue from a large European manufacturing client.
Additional onsite mix reduction of 0.75%–1% and similar third-party costs to FY26 levels are expected.
Guidance range is narrower than last year due to better clarity on the environment and client behavior.

guidance_ledger.statement: Revenue guidance is 1.5%–3.5% CC YoY, with the lower end assuming higher deterioration in the environment and the upper end assuming an improved environment.
Operating margin guidance is 20%–22%.
0.75%–1% revenue reduction due to lower revenue from a large European manufacturing client.
Expected additional onsite mix reduction of 0.75%–1%.
The opportunity is between $300 bn and $400 bn in the year getting to 2030.

risk_register.note: Management acknowledged a change in economic environment after conflict escalation, but sees paths towards stabilization.
Management acknowledged occasional competitor proposals outside the range of what models can deliver.
CEO described Mythos as exposing more vulnerabilities than expected, framing it as a services opportunity.
CEO gave 'No comment' on tenure/succession-related queries.
Companies need effective change management to leverage AI opportunities.
The gap between AI technology advancement and enterprise implementation capabilities.
Increasing breaches with AI used by state and non-state actors.
The results for Q3 FY'26 include a charge associated with change in labor codes in India, impacting operating profit, net profit, EPS, and free cash flow.
The environment remains uncertain with macroeconomic challenges impacting client spending.
Majority of U.S. workforce does not require Infosys sponsorship for immigration.

themes.label: AI Deployment
themes.note: Infosys reiterated its AI thesis and highlighted production-scale examples and partnerships.

themes.label: Legacy Modernization
themes.note: AI-led transformation and legacy modernization are key growth drivers in Financial Services.

themes.label: Talent Transformation
themes.note: Reinvestment in talent and AI skills development is ongoing, with a focus on AI-aligned skills.

themes.label: Strategic Partnerships
themes.note: New partnerships with OpenAI, Anthropic, and others enhance AI capabilities.

themes.label: Execution Risk
themes.note: Geopolitical tensions and competitive pressures pose risks, but management sees paths to stabilization.

key_takeaways: Revenue growth for FY26 was 3.1% in constant currency, with Q4 growth at 4.1% YoY and a decline of 1.3% QoQ due to seasonality and slower decision making.
FY26 revenues crossed $20 billion, with strong performance in Financial Services, Communications, Manufacturing, and Europe.
Large deals totaled $14.9–$15.0 billion in FY26, a 28% YoY increase, with 55% net new.
Operating margin for FY26 was 21%, with Q4 at 20.9%, and gains reinvested in talent, AI, and sales and marketing.
FY27 revenue guidance is 1.5%–3.5% CC YoY, with operating margin guidance at 20%–22%.
AI strategy focuses on a large addressable market across six areas, with core platforms Topaz Fabric and Cobalt operational.
Management highlighted AI-driven growth and strategic partnerships, while managing risks from geopolitical tensions and competitive pressures.

quarters.summary: Infosys is focusing on AI-driven transformation across its services, emphasizing modernization, talent transformation, and strategic partnerships to leverage AI opportunities.

subsections.label: Tech Transitions
points: AI transition is faster than previous tech shifts, reaching 1 billion users in a couple of years compared to 10 years for the Internet and 5 years for smartphones.
AI is a fundamental change in business operations, requiring AI-native architecture and changes in customer journeys.
Modernization of legacy systems is crucial to leverage AI, as many large companies spend 60% to 80% of IT budgets on maintenance.
AI allows for faster and more economical modernization, addressing accumulated tech debt.
The balance of advantage is shifting towards 'build' rather than 'buy' due to AI's simplicity in building applications.
Foundational systems will become systems of record with agentic interfaces, simplifying user interaction.
The pace of AI change is unprecedented, with models growing from 100 billion to 1 trillion parameters in a few years.
AI technology is advancing faster than enterprise deployment capabilities, creating a deployment gap.

subsections.label: AI Services Strategy
points: Infosys is trusted by clients for AI work, with AI services contributing 5.5% of revenue in Q3.
The AI-first value framework identifies six growth areas: AI strategy and engineering, data for AI, process transformation, legacy modernization, physical AI, and AI trust.
Infosys is executing AI projects for 90% of its top 200 clients, using a Hexagon framework to structure AI services.
The company is focusing on AI strategy and engineering, data readiness, process transformation, legacy modernization, physical AI, and AI trust.
Infosys is leveraging partnerships with major AI players and open-source models to enhance AI services.
The company is reskilling its workforce, hiring 20,000 college graduates annually, and focusing on AI training.

subsections.label: Strategic Partnerships and Initiatives
points: Infosys is forming strategic partnerships, such as a joint venture with Telstra for B2B non-connectivity solutions in Australia.
The company is leveraging partnerships with AI-native partners and universities for joint research and innovation.
Infosys is focusing on AI-first and AI-augmented services, integrating AI into traditional services and creating new AI-driven offerings.
The company is working with partners like Microsoft to build IT systems and enhance cloud services.
Infosys is using its Topaz Fabric to integrate IP and platforms, providing clients with modular and composable AI solutions.

subsections.label: Execution Risk
points: There is an execution risk in AI deployment, as companies need effective change management to leverage AI opportunities.
The deployment gap between AI technology advancement and enterprise implementation capabilities poses a challenge.
Security breaches are increasing, with AI being used by state and non-state actors, highlighting the need for robust security measures.

subsections.label: AI Opportunity and Strategy
points: Infosys sees a large opportunity in AI services, with an estimated market size of $300 billion to $400 billion by 2030.
The company is focusing on AI-first services, AI-augmented services, and foundational components to drive growth.
Infosys is committed to reskilling its workforce and recruiting new talent to support AI initiatives.

guidance_ledger.statement: The opportunity is between $300 bn and $400 bn in the year getting to 2030.
Revenue growth guidance for the financial year is 3% to 3.5% growth in constant currency.
Operating margin guidance for the financial year remains the same at 20% to 22%.

risk_register.note: Companies need effective change management to leverage AI opportunities.
The gap between AI technology advancement and enterprise implementation capabilities.
Increasing breaches with AI used by state and non-state actors.
The results for Q3 FY'26 include a charge associated with change in labor codes in India, impacting operating profit, net profit, EPS, and free cash flow.
The environment remains uncertain with macroeconomic challenges impacting client spending.
Majority of U.S. workforce does not require Infosys sponsorship for immigration.

themes.label: AI Deployment
themes.note: Infosys is executing AI projects for 90% of its top 200 clients, using a Hexagon framework to structure AI services.

themes.label: Legacy Modernization
themes.note: Modernization of legacy systems is crucial to leverage AI, addressing accumulated tech debt.

themes.label: Talent Transformation
themes.note: Infosys is reskilling its workforce and hiring 20,000 college graduates annually for AI training.

themes.label: Strategic Partnerships
themes.note: Infosys is forming strategic partnerships and leveraging AI-native partners for innovation.

themes.label: Execution Risk
themes.note: There is an execution risk in AI deployment, requiring effective change management.

key_takeaways: AI transition is faster than previous tech shifts, reaching 1 billion users in a couple of years compared to 10 years for the Internet and 5 years for smartphones.
AI is a fundamental change in business operations, requiring AI-native architecture and changes in customer journeys.
Modernization of legacy systems is crucial to leverage AI, as many large companies spend 60% to 80% of IT budgets on maintenance.
Infosys is trusted by clients for AI work, with AI services contributing 5.5% of revenue in Q3.
Infosys sees a large opportunity in AI services, with an estimated market size of $300 billion to $400 billion by 2030.
There is an execution risk in AI deployment, as companies need effective change management to leverage AI opportunities.
The deployment gap between AI technology advancement and enterprise implementation capabilities poses a challenge.

quarters.summary: Infosys has revised its revenue growth guidance upwards to 3% to 3.5% for FY26, driven by strong large deal wins and AI adoption, while maintaining its operating margin guidance at 20% to 22%.

subsections.label: Margin Analysis
points: Adjusted operating margins increased by 20 basis points sequentially to 21.2%.
Operating margins including the impact of change in labor codes stands at 18.4%.
The puts and takes of 20 basis point expansion this quarter include 40 basis points from currency, 50 basis points from Project Maximus, offset by furloughs and lower working days.

subsections.label: Cash Flow and Capital Allocation
points: Free cash flow generation adjusted for labor codes remained robust at $965 mn, which is 113% of adjusted net profit.
During the quarter, Infosys completed its largest ever buyback, returning INR18,000 crores to shareholders.

key_figures.label: Total Large Deal TCV for 9 months

subsections.label: Large Deals
points: Large deal TCV was robust at $4.8 bn in Q3 with 57% net new.
Total large deal TCV for 9 months stood at $11.7 bn, exceeding the total large deal TCV of full year FY'25.
Net new deal TCV for 9 months was up by 40% as compared to the same period last year.
Large deal pipeline continues to be healthy.

subsections.label: Vertical Performance
points: Momentum in Financial Services continues with 3.9% year-on-year growth in constant currency terms.
Among geos, Europe continued to lead the growth by 7.2% year-on-year in constant currency terms.
Healthcare benefited from the contribution from the NHS deal.

key_figures.label: Lines of Code Generated Using AI

subsections.label: AI Initiatives
points: Infosys has deepened its Topaz AI capability with an agent services suite called Topaz Fabric.
Infosys is working on 4,600 AI projects and has generated over 28 mn lines of code using AI.
Infosys has built over 500 AI agents and is scaling its forward deployed engineer team.
Infosys is witnessing six AI-led value pools emerging that could unlock a large incremental opportunity.

subsections.label: AI Adoption
points: Infosys works with 90% of its largest 200 clients to unlock value with AI.
Infosys is a preferred AI partner for top 15 out of 25 banking clients.

subsections.label: Partnerships and Acquisitions
points: Infosys announced a partnership with Cognition to combine Cognition's Devin software agent with Infosys' knowledge of the client landscape and industry expertise.
Infosys has a good pipeline of possible companies for acquisition and discussions.

subsections.label: Labor Code Impact
points: The results for Q3 FY'26 include a charge associated with change in labor codes in India, impacting operating profit, net profit, EPS, and free cash flow.
The ongoing impact of the labor code will be roughly around 15 basis points annually.

subsections.label: Revised Guidance
points: Infosys has revised its revenue growth guidance for the financial year to 3% to 3.5% growth in constant currency.
Operating margin guidance for the financial year remains the same at 20% to 22%.

guidance_ledger.statement: Revenue growth guidance for the financial year is 3% to 3.5% growth in constant currency.
Operating margin guidance for the financial year remains the same at 20% to 22%.

risk_register.note: The results for Q3 FY'26 include a charge associated with change in labor codes in India, impacting operating profit, net profit, EPS, and free cash flow.
The environment remains uncertain with macroeconomic challenges impacting client spending.
Majority of U.S. workforce does not require Infosys sponsorship for immigration.

themes.label: AI Deployment
themes.note: Infosys is expanding its AI capabilities with Topaz Fabric and partnerships like Cognition.

themes.label: Large Deal Momentum
themes.note: Infosys reported a robust large deal TCV of $4.8 bn in Q3 with 57% net new.

themes.label: Talent Acquisition
themes.note: Infosys increased its headcount by 5,000 to 337,000 employees in Q3 FY26.

themes.label: Macroeconomic Uncertainty
themes.note: Infosys is navigating uncertainties with revised guidance and strategic initiatives.

themes.label: H-1B Visa Strategy
themes.note: Infosys continues its approach with a mix of U.S. and India-based workforces.

key_takeaways: Infosys has revised its revenue growth guidance for the financial year to 3% to 3.5% growth in constant currency.
Operating margin guidance for the financial year remains the same at 20% to 22%.
Large deal TCV was robust at $4.8 bn in Q3 with 57% net new.
Infosys works with 90% of its largest 200 clients to unlock value with AI.
Infosys announced a partnership with Cognition to combine Cognition's Devin software agent with Infosys' knowledge of the client landscape and industry expertise.
The results for Q3 FY'26 include a charge associated with change in labor codes in India, impacting operating profit, net profit, EPS, and free cash flow.
Infosys has deepened its Topaz AI capability with an agent services suite called Topaz Fabric.

quarters.summary: Infosys reported strong Q2 FY26 performance with revenue growth and increased guidance, driven by large deals and AI initiatives, despite macroeconomic uncertainties.

subsections.label: Quarterly Performance
points: Revenues for the quarter grew 2.2% sequentially and 2.9% year-on-year in constant currency terms.
Operating margin was 21%.
Free cash flow stood at $1.1 billion, which is 131% of the net profit.
EPS in rupee terms grew by 13% year-on-year to Rs. 17.6.

subsections.label: Cash Flow and Investments
points: Consolidated cash and investments were at $6.2 billion at the end of the quarter.
Yield on cash balance was at 6.98% and ROE stood at 29.1%.

key_figures.label: Net New Work in Large Deals

subsections.label: Deal Activity
points: Large deals were at $3.1 billion with 67% net new.
A mega deal worth $1.6 billion was announced after the close of the quarter.

subsections.label: Industry Performance
points: Financial Services and Manufacturing grew above 5% year-on-year in constant currency.
Retail clients remain cautious due to tariff-related uncertainties.

subsections.label: Regional Performance
points: Europe grew greater than 5% year-on-year in constant currency terms.
The U.S. market remains a strong focus for growth and investment.

subsections.label: Hiring and Talent Strategy
points: Added 8,000 employees during the quarter.
Hired over 12,000 freshers in the last 6 months.

subsections.label: Acquisitions and Joint Ventures
points: Announced a joint venture with Versent in Australia, pending regulatory approvals.
Focus on strategic acquisitions to enhance capabilities in digital, AI, and cloud.

subsections.label: Macroeconomic Uncertainty
points: The environment remains uncertain with macroeconomic challenges impacting client spending.

subsections.label: H-1B Visa Issue
points: Majority of U.S. workforce does not require Infosys sponsorship for immigration.
No specific numbers shared on H-1B visa dependency.

subsections.label: Guidance Update
points: Revenue growth guidance for the financial year is 2% to 3% in constant currency terms.
Operating margin guidance remains at 20% to 22% for the full year.

guidance_ledger.statement: Revenue growth guidance for the financial year is 2% to 3% in constant currency terms.
Operating margin guidance remains at 20% to 22% for the full year.

risk_register.note: The environment remains uncertain with macroeconomic challenges impacting client spending.
Majority of U.S. workforce does not require Infosys sponsorship for immigration.

themes.label: AI Deployment
themes.note: Strong focus on deploying AI across enterprises for growth and cost efficiency.

themes.label: Large Deal Momentum
themes.note: Continued strong performance in securing large deals, including a mega deal.

themes.label: Macroeconomic Uncertainty
themes.note: Uncertain macro environment impacting client spending and guidance.

themes.label: H-1B Visa Strategy
themes.note: Majority of U.S. workforce does not require Infosys sponsorship for immigration.

themes.label: Talent Acquisition
themes.note: Significant hiring of freshers and overall employee additions.

key_takeaways: Revenues for the quarter grew 2.2% sequentially and 2.9% year-on-year in constant currency terms.
Operating margin was 21%.
Large deals were at $3.1 billion with 67% net new.
A mega deal worth $1.6 billion was announced after the close of the quarter.
Revenue growth guidance for the financial year is 2% to 3% in constant currency terms.
Operating margin guidance remains at 20% to 22% for the full year.
Added 8,000 employees during the quarter.
Hired over 12,000 freshers in the last 6 months.
